name: j-mcp
description: Embed the J array programming language as MCP tools — named sessions, evaluate sentences, tokenize, interrupt, and even register new MCP tools whose body is a J verb.
long_description: Spawns and manages J interpreter sessions via libj. The client can create independent named sessions, evaluate sentences, read and write dense arrays (int/char/bool/float, incl. UTF-16 and UTF-32 chars), tokenize, and interrupt a running sentence. An LLM can also register its own MCP tools whose implementation is a J verb — those tools are persisted to disk and replayed into their backing session on reconnect.

jhome.title: J install directory
jhome.description: Path to the directory that contains libj (libj.so / libj.dylib / j.dll) and profile.ijs — typically your J install's bin folder (e.g. C:\Program Files\J9.6\bin on Windows, /Applications/J9.6/bin on macOS, /usr/lib/j9 or ~/j/bin on Linux).

tools.name: j_session_restart
tools.description: Terminate and recreate a session by name.

tools.name: j_eval
tools.description: Evaluate a J sentence in a session.

tools.name: j_parse
tools.description: Tokenize a sentence via J's ;: primitive.

tools.name: j_get
tools.description: Read a J noun as a shape+type+data payload (int/char/bool/float).

tools.name: j_set
tools.description: Create a J noun from a shape+type+data payload.

tools.name: j_tool_register
tools.description: Register a new MCP tool whose implementation is a J verb.

tools.name: j_tool_unregister
tools.description: Drop a previously registered J-defined tool.
